content.problemStatement:
Given an array of N intervals in the form of (start[i], end[i]), where start[i] is the starting point of the interval and end[i] is the ending point of the interval, return the minimum number 
    of intervals that need to be removed to make the remaining intervals non-overlapping. .
constraints: Intervals are given as a list of lists of two integers each
content.inputFormat: Intervals = [[start1, end1], [start2, end2], ... , [startN, endN]] where starti and endi are integers
content.outputFormat: Minimum number of intervals to remove

sampleTestCases.explanation: You can remove the interval [1, 3] to make the remaining intervals non-overlapping.
You can remove the intervals [1, 4] and [3, 5] to make the rest non-overlapping.

solution.approach: Sort intervals by end time and then use a greedy approach to select non-overlapping intervals
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) due to sorting
